dependencies: Reliance on third-party transportation providers including contracted motor carriers, railroads, ocean carriers, and air carriers
Dependence on 450,000 transportation providers on platform
No ownership of transportation equipment - relies entirely on contracted capacity
Single-source dependencies on certain transportation modes in specific lanes
risks: Equipment and driver shortages in the transportation industry, particularly among contracted motor carriers
Disruption in the supply or cost of fuel
Reduction or deterioration in rail service
Geopolitical factors that may limit the availability of certain carriers
Port congestion and availability of transportation equipment
Concentration risk - largest truck transportation provider less than 1% of total cost, but reliance on many small carriers (73% of truckload shipments from carriers with fewer than 100 trucks)

regulatory_risks: U.S. Department of Transportation (DOT) licensing and regulation as property freight broker
Federal Maritime Commission (FMC) regulation as ocean transportation intermediary (NVOCC and freight forwarder)
Transportation Security Administration (TSA) regulations for Indirect Air Carrier (IAC) operations
U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) customs brokerage licensing and CTPAT certification requirements
U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) licensing under Perishable Agricultural Commodities Act (PACA)
Federal and state safety and environmental regulations affecting contracted motor carriers
State economic regulation of motor carriers and brokers (partially preempted by federal law)
Federal and state food and drug statutes and regulations for sourcing business
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and UK Bribery Act 2010 compliance
European General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and California Consumer Privacy Act data privacy requirements
U.S. Department of Homeland Security regulations for importers and ocean carriers
Environmental regulations protecting the environment, including transitional risks relating to climate change
OECD Pillar Two Model Rules - global minimum tax of 15% for multinational enterprises (effective 2024 in EU, expanding 2025)
Changes in tariffs, trade restrictions, and trade agreements
Sanctions compliance (Iran, Russia, other sanctioned countries)
Changes to income tax regulations in U.S. and international jurisdictions

sanction_risks: Red Sea conflict impacting ocean freight routes and causing carriers to avoid Suez Canal throughout 2024
Geopolitical factors limiting availability of certain carriers
Sanctions compliance requirements under FCPA and similar regulations
Argentina operations divested in 2023 due to deteriorating economic conditions and political instability
Foreign currency risk exposure including Chinese Yuan, Euro, Singapore Dollar, Mexican Peso, Hong Kong Dollar, Polish Zloty, Australian Dollar
Wars, civil unrest, acts of terrorism impacting operations
Changes in political conditions and governmental policies
International compliance risks in multiple jurisdictions
